description_long: This bungalow consists of two main rectangular structures connected by a covered bridge over a small circular water feature. The left building is taller, with three visible stories, large windows, and a rooftop terrace, while the right building is a single-story structure with a sloped roof and multiple windows. Both buildings are set on landscaped green patches with surrounding walkways, and the bridge provides a unique architectural connection between the two. The exterior surfaces are primarily light-colored, with accents of gray and blue, and the windows are generously sized, allowing for ample natural light. The overall design is modern, with clean lines, flat and sloped roof elements, and minimal ornamentation. The setting suggests a private, upscale residential property with a focus on open space and connectivity.
description: A modern two-part bungalow with a tall main building, a single-story annex, and a connecting bridge over a water feature, set on landscaped grounds with large windows and clean lines.
description_view_0: Front view showing both the tall main building and the single-story annex connected by a bridge, with landscaped areas visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the bridge connection and the rear of the main building, with the annex extending to the right.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the single-story annex and the bridge, with the main building in the background.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the multi-story main building, rooftop terrace, and the bridge leading to the annex.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large grouped windows and scattered placements
